title: Tab Key Behavior
text: Tab Key Behavior The default (USYS) keyboard translation tables define the following behavior for the Tab key: When not in Zoom mode: Tab is defined as ^FIELD. ^FIELD is defined as ^NEXT_FIELD or ^PREV_FIELD. GOLD Tab is defined as a normal tab character. When in Zoom mode: Tab is defined as the ^HT (horizontal tab) character. ^FIELD is defined as the ^HT character. In Zoom mode, Uniface converts any ^FIELD definition to a ^HT character since it is then impossible to go to the previous or next field. In this way, the Tab key can be used for both the functions to which computer users are accustomed. Note: The definitions for Tab and ^FIELD can be changed if required. If you do change the behavior, remember to make the definitions after the mapping of ^GOLD_BASE_SET . Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
